candidates.why: Primary operating revenue line; matches top-line rental income for a property company and equals Total Income numerically in preview, indicating it is the base revenue component.
Includes Rental income and potentially other operating income; kept as secondary fallback line in case label usage changes over time.
Represents operating profit, not revenue, so not appropriate as REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue.
Interest income, not operating revenue.
Derivative interest income, not operating revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Bottom-line comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Net income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Owners of Parent Company - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income to minorities, not revenue.
Aggregate comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Basic and Diluted Total Comprehensive Income per share (USD)
candidates.why: Per-share comprehensive income, not revenue.

variables.notes: Rental income is the primary operating revenue line and matches the Total Income values, indicating Total Income is a subtotal including Rental income; both are provided as a priority list for REVT as labels can change over time.

candidates.why: Direct costs associated with rental properties; most appropriate analog to cost of goods sold/cost of sales for a property company.
Overhead SG&A, not direct cost of sales.
Overhead personnel costs, typically SG&A rather than COGS for a property investment entity.
General operating overhead; better treated as SG&A.
Likely another overhead operating expense line, not clearly direct cost of sales.
Financing costs, not cost of goods sold.
Financing-related, not COGS.
Balance sheet liability, not an income statement COGS line.

variables.notes: For a rental property company, Property expenses are the direct costs associated with generating rental income and best represent COGS/cost of sales; other listed expenses are operating overhead or financing costs.

candidates.why: This is income, not expense, so not SG&A expense; excluded.
Classic administrative overhead expense; clear SG&A component.
Personnel-related overhead; clear SG&A component when not included in COGS.
General operating overhead not classified as COGS; good SG&A component.

variables.notes: SG&A is captured via the main overhead components: Administration expenses, Personnel Expenses, and Other operating expenses. Income lines and totals are excluded per instructions.
No research & development-related line items are present in the provided Income Statement or Balance Sheet previews, so XRD is set to 0 (no mapping).

candidates.why: Primary income statement line for interest expense; best match for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Specific subset of interest expense related to lease liabilities; good secondary fallback.
Represents minority interest share of profit, not interest expense.
Generic interest expense line (likely supplemental); good secondary fallback if populated when main Interest Expenses is missing.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease-related interest; lower-priority fallback.
Balance sheet liability, not an expense line.
Balance sheet debt balance, not an expense line.
Non-current debt, not an expense line.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Interest expense is mapped to the main Income Statement interest lines, ordered by generality and likelihood of consistent reporting; balance sheet debt items and minority interest are excluded.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution of profit, not a balance sheet equity amount.
Component of equity but not the full book equity; preferable to use a total equity figure.
Component of equity only.
Component/line within equity, not total.
Represents total shareholders’ equity; no separate parent/owner vs non-controlling split is provided, so this is the best BE proxy.
Duplicate total equity label with different capitalization; using one avoids duplication in final_choice.

variables.notes: No separate line for equity attributable to owners vs non-controlling interests appears in the balance sheet; Total equity is therefore used as BE, and MIB will be set to 0 to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Income statement attribution of continuing operations profit to minorities, not a balance sheet equity amount.
Income statement total minority interest in profit, not the book value of minority equity.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attributable to minorities, not balance sheet minority interest.

variables.notes: No balance sheet line for non-controlling interests/minority interest is present; income statement minority interest lines are not appropriate for book MIB. Since BE uses Total equity, MIB is set to 0 to avoid double counting.
